name: Aphrodite Engine
tagline: High-throughput LLM inference engine built on vLLM's PagedAttention
metaDescription: Aphrodite Engine serves HuggingFace-compatible LLMs at scale with PagedAttention, continuous batching, wide quantization support, and an OpenAI-compatible API.
overview: Aphrodite Engine is an inference engine for serving HuggingFace-compatible language models to many concurrent users. It is built on vLLM's PagedAttention for K/V cache management and adds continuous batching and optimized CUDA kernels to keep throughput high under load.

It is aimed at teams and developers who need to run open LLMs themselves rather than calling a hosted API. Launching a model starts an OpenAI-compatible server, so existing OpenAI client code and UIs like SillyTavern can connect without changes. Aphrodite is the backend that powers PygmalionAI's chat platforms.

As a model-serving tool, it stands out for its broad quantization coverage, supporting formats such as AWQ, GPTQ, GGUF, Bitsandbytes, ExLlamaV3, and more, which lets you fit larger models onto limited GPU memory.
features: Continuous batching and PagedAttention from vLLM for efficient K/V cache management
Wide quantization support: AWQ, GPTQ, GGUF, Bitsandbytes, ExLlamaV3, AQLM, Marlin, FP8, and more
OpenAI-compatible API server out of the box, usable with tools like SillyTavern
Distributed and disaggregated inference for serving at scale
Speculative decoding (EAGLE, ngram, MTP, and others) plus multimodal and multi-LoRA support
Modern samplers including DRY, XTC, and Mirostat, with a quantized FP8 KV cache

gettingStarted.intro: Install the engine with pip, then launch any HuggingFace-compatible model to start an OpenAI-compatible API server on localhost port 2242.

steps.heading: Install the engine
steps.body: Install from PyPI. Requires Linux or Windows (WSL2), Python 3.10 to 3.13, and CUDA 12 or newer.

steps.heading: Launch a model
steps.body: Run a model by its HuggingFace repo name. This starts an OpenAI-compatible API server on port 2242 of localhost.

steps.heading: Limit GPU memory if needed
steps.body: By default Aphrodite uses about 92% of GPU VRAM. To cap it (for example to 60%), pass the gpu-memory-utilization flag. Run aphrodite run --help to see all options.

useCases: Self-host an open LLM behind an OpenAI-compatible API so existing OpenAI client code keeps working
Serve a quantized model (AWQ, GPTQ, GGUF, and others) to fit a larger model onto limited GPU memory
Run a high-throughput backend for a chat platform or app handling many concurrent users
Power local UIs such as SillyTavern by pointing them at the engine's OpenAI endpoint
